documentation.value: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.

A URI reference [RFC3986] that identifies the problem type. This specification encourages that, when dereferenced, it provide human-readable documentation for the problem type (e.g., using HTML [W3C.REC-html5-20141028]). When this member is not present, its value is assumed to be "about:blank".

A short, human-readable summary of the problem type. It SHOULD NOT change from occurrence to occurrence of the problem, except for purposes of localization (e.g., using proactive content negotiation; see [RFC7231], Section 3.4).
The HTTP status code ([RFC7231], Section 6) generated by the origin server for this occurrence of the problem.
A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem. It may or may not yield further information if dereferenced.

properties.documentation: An ISO 8601 standard representation of a date.
An ISO 8601 standard representation of a date & time.
A representation of a Uniform Resource Identifier as defined in IETF RFC 3986.
A Uniform Resource Locator (URL) as defined in IETF RFC 1738.
A string representation of a Universally Unique Identifier (UUID) as defined in IETF RFC 9562.
Initial version of Primitive types library.
A representation of objects that can have two values: true and false.
A signed 8-bit integer, ranges from -128 to 127
A signed 32-bit integer, ranges from --2,147,483,648 to 2,147,483,647.
A generic number type that can represent both integer and decimal numbers.
